# task\-graph\-fix\-crash\-and\-duplicates — Fix loadGraphData crash on malformed JSON and deduplicate impact hints
status: done  priority: 1  scope: scope-core
readiness: not_eligible  reason: status_done

Two bugs found in src/core/graph\.ts during audit:

1\. \*\*loadGraphData crashes on malformed JSON\*\* — Line 104: \`JSON\.parse\(readFileSync\(\.\.\.\)\)\` is not wrapped in try/catch\. If graph\.json exists but contains invalid JSON, buildBrief crashes instead of degrading gracefully\. This violates the spec requirement that briefs should always succeed even when graph data is missing or invalid\.

2\. \*\*findImpactHints produces duplicate hints\*\* — When multiple path\_hints match the same node \(e\.g\. "src/core/brief\.ts" and "src/core/"\), the inbound/outbound processing runs twice for that node, adding identical hint strings\. Need to track processed nodes to avoid duplicates\.

Plus two dead\-code items:
\- graph\_data\_invalid in result\.ts:215 is never referenced \(can remove or use it\)
\- \!graph\.concepts check in findRelevantConcepts is always false after remapGraph \(concepts is always an array\)

## Acceptance
- loadGraphData returns empty graph when graph\.json contains invalid JSON \(no crash\)\.
- findImpactHints does not produce duplicate hint strings when multiple path\_hints match the same node\.
- Add test: loadGraphData handles malformed JSON gracefully\.
- Add test: findImpactHints deduplicates when path\_hints overlap\.
- All existing tests still pass\.
- typecheck, biome check, and waystation validate all clean\.
